description: Letter from a penniless woman, the widow of Abū Surrī, to Mevorakh b. Saadya (1094–1111). She begs him to come to her rescue in a litigation brought against her by the relatives of her deceased son-in-law for a modest amount. Her daughter was married to Yosef b. Asad b. Qirqas who left her to travel three and a half years ago. That was prior to al-Afḍal's siege of Alexandria in 1094. The daughter was then ill two years while the mother used her dowry (רחלהא) for nursing her in her illness and for the burial when she died. It has recently become known that Yosef was killed in Nastaro (an island between Damietta and Alexandria), and his cousin claimed his estate—which was non-existent. (Information from Goitein's note card and from M. R. Cohen, Jewish Self Government, pp. 221-260.) For a detailed discussion of the geographical situation of Nastaro, see Khan, "A Copy of a Decree from the Archives of the Fāṭimid Chancery in Egypt," BSOAS, Vol. 49, No. 3, 1986, p. 444.
tags: Illness: women's, funeral, al-Malik al-Afdal (1094-1121), siege, illness letter 969-1517, Illness, illness: cost, illness: nursing, communal, Petition, Women's letters, illness: poverty, 11th c
description_he: מכתב מאישה חסרת אמצעים, אלמנת אבו סורי, למבורך ב' סעדיה (1094–1111). היא מתחננת להצילה מתביעה שהגישו קרובי חתנה המנוח. בתה נישאה ליוסף ב' אסד ב' קרקס שעזב לנסוע לפני שלוש וחצי שנים. הבת חלתה ומתה, ויוסף נהרג באי ביי נסתארו.
